result:
Query surfaced ~15 relevant items (several raw chunks + 8 crystallized notes); crystallized 0 new lessons — the vault already carries a fully recency-linked thread on exactly this question, so there was no un-covered principle to write.

**Memory overturned my Step-0 instinct.** I was leaning toward "cheapen recall itself" (glance mode / fewer than 10 phrases / payload-size cap). Memory explicitly kills all three:

- Walking my plan: step 3's candidate lever is **contradicted**. Cutting the 10 query phrases is DEAD/refuted — "breadth surfaces the un-guessable notes" (note 100). Payload-size cap for dollars is DEAD — payload is cheap `cache_read` and capping it −61% left end-to-end cost flat (notes 77, 79, 100). Whole-op/split-haiku was only −14% and broke half the builds (notes 80, 100). So the "make recall cheap" family is a measured dead end.
- Steps 1–2, 4 (sweep, query, reconcile): **confirmed** — this is exactly the retrieval-then-test-against-memory path the surfaced notes reward.

**Apply these as requirements (recency-weighted, newest wins):**

1. **The build/task loop is ~80% of op dollars; recall is only ~20%** (note 102's $METER baseline; note 77). Any lever that only touches recall is capped at a fifth of the bill. So the highest-leverage lever must target the *build* loop.
2. **The biggest whole-op dollar lever is model-tier routing** (note 136, 2026-06-28, which *narrows* both note 77 and note 100). Because **memory democratizes reasoning** — sonnet-with-memory measured == opus-warm on apply-conventions, recency-supersession, and emergent-abduction (note 135) — you can drop the memory-backed operation one capability tier for ~25–30%/trial with quality held. This shipped 2026-06-28 and the re-entry check found **no rollback**.
3. The runner-up — **isolating the recall payload out of build context** (measured build_cost −40%, note 149) — is *bigger in isolated build terms* but its production build was **REJECTED 2026-07-08** as too complicated (it required launching from engram via `claude -p` rather than as a subagent); reopen only on a viable subagent route (note 191). So it is not the actionable single change today.

Net: the operation in front of me (this smoke harness) runs `model="opus"`. Given memory democratizes the reasoning, the single highest-leverage, validated, *unblocked* change is to route the memory-backed op to the cheapest capability tier that still passes the trap gate — not to run it on opus. Guardrail (note 100/102): any such cut must pass the C3/C4i/C5/C6 trap gate and be measured on the unbundled recall_cost/build_cost $METER before shipping.

Re-entry: route memory-backed reasoning to a cheaper model tier — clean (top hits: 135/136, 2026-06-28, shipped, not rolled back).
Re-entry: isolate recall payload out of build context — CLOSED (191.2026-07-08): production build rejected 2026-07-08, reopen only on a viable subagent route → drop as the top pick (measured benefit stands but blocked).

RECOMMENDATION: Route the memory-backed operation to the cheapest capability tier that still passes the trap gate (memory-democratized reasoning: sonnet-with-memory matches opus) instead of running it on opus — the build loop is ~80% of op dollars, so tiering it down is the largest validated, unblocked whole-op dollar cut.
